Cabbage_Alternia_Leaf_Spot.cause: Fungus (Alternaria brassicicola) mostly due to high humidity.
Cabbage_Alternia_Leaf_Spot.remedy: Use copper-based fungicides and avoid overhead watering.

Cabbage_Aphid_Colony.cause: Small insects (Aphids) sucking sap from the leaves.
Cabbage_Aphid_Colony.remedy: Spray neem oil or use a strong water stream to wash them off.

Cabbage_Healthy.remedy: Maintain regular watering and proper sunlight.

Cabbage_Ring_Spot.cause: Fungal infection causing circular spots with dark borders.
Cabbage_Ring_Spot.remedy: Remove infected leaves and apply balanced fertilizer.

Malabar_Healthy.remedy: Provide a trellis for climbing and keep soil moist.

Malabar_Anthracnose.cause: Fungal pathogen causing dark, sunken lesions.
Malabar_Anthracnose.remedy: Improve air circulation and use fungicide if necessary.

Malabar_Bactorial_Spot.cause: Bacterial infection spreading through water splashes.
Malabar_Bactorial_Spot.remedy: Avoid touching plants when wet; use bactericides.

Malabar_PestDamage.cause: Local insects or beetles feeding on leaves.
Malabar_PestDamage.remedy: Use organic pest control or hand-pick larger pests.

Potato_EarlyBlight.cause: Fungus (Alternaria solani) during warm, humid weather.

Potato_Healthy.remedy: Ensure proper hilling and nitrogen balance.

Potato_LaterBlight.remedy: Immediately remove infected plants and spray specialized fungicides.

Radish_Black_Leaf_Spot.cause: Fungal infection causing small dark spots on radish leaves.
Radish_Black_Leaf_Spot.remedy: Rotate crops and use disease-free seeds.

Radish_Flea_Beetle.cause: Small beetles creating tiny 'shotholes' in leaves.
Radish_Flea_Beetle.remedy: Use row covers or apply neem-based insecticides.

Radish_Healthy.remedy: Keep soil loose and weed-free for good root growth.

Radish_Mosaic_Virus.cause: Viral infection often spread by aphids.
Radish_Mosaic_Virus.remedy: Control aphids; infected plants must be removed and destroyed.
